description:
You are a platform-engineer reviewing a legacy directory of Kubernetes manifests that sits at:

/home/user/manifests

Your goal is to batch-upgrade every Deployment manifest that still hard-codes the generic Docker Hub image “nginx” so that it now pins the version tag “1.21”.

Constraint:
• You must solve it only with the classic Unix combo of ​find + xargs (you may combine them with grep, sed, awk, etc.).  
• A correct solution is expected to need roughly 5-10 individual shell commands.

Step-by-step requirements

1. Locate every file anywhere under /home/user/manifests whose basename ends in “.yaml” or “.yml” AND that contains the literal line “image: nginx” (exact spelling, single space after the colon).  
2. In each of those files replace that exact line with “image: nginx:1.21”.  
   • No other content may be altered.  
   • Files that already reference “nginx:” with any tag or digest must be left untouched.
3. Create a new directory exactly named /home/user/manifest_update.  
4. In that directory create a plain-text file named update.log that lists, one per line, all RELATIVE paths (relative to /home/user/manifests) of the files you actually modified, followed by a single space and the word “UPDATED”.  
   Example line format that the grader will look for (do NOT prepend “./”):  
   ns1/dep-nginx.yaml UPDATED
5. In the same directory create summary.txt that contains nothing except the total count of modified files as an ASCII decimal number followed by a single trailing newline (Unix LF). No extra spaces or text.
6. When you are done, print the full contents of update.log to STDOUT so a human can see it.

What the autograder will verify

• Each of the originally “image: nginx” lines was changed to “image: nginx:1.21”.  
• No other file lines changed.  
• /home/user/manifest_update/update.log exists with exactly one correctly-formatted line per modified file, order unimportant.  
• /home/user/manifest_update/summary.txt exists and holds the correct integer.  
• Both files have default user-writable permissions (0600 – 0666 range is fine).  
• You reached the goal using find and xargs somewhere in your session history (the grader will replay the bash history).

Hints (not commands)

• Use find’s -type f, -name, and -print0 flags together with xargs -0 to stay safe with spaces.  
• grep -l or -Z can help you feed candidate file names to xargs.  
• sed -i … or similar streaming editor can perform the in-place substitution.  
• Remember to cd into /home/user/manifests before generating the relative paths for update.log.
truth:
Initial directory tree and file contents BEFORE the agent starts:

/home/user/manifests/
├── dep_proxy.yaml
├── ns1/
│   ├── dep-nginx.yaml
│   └── svc.yaml
├── ns2/
│   ├── dep-api.yaml
│   └── dep-nginx-2.yml
└── ns3/
    └── job.yaml


1. /home/user/manifests/dep_proxy.yaml  (12 lines total)

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-proxy
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx-proxy
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx-proxy
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: proxy
        image: nginx
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80


2. /home/user/manifests/ns1/dep-nginx.yaml  (15 lines total)

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80


3. /home/user/manifests/ns1/svc.yaml  (service manifest with no “image:” line – must stay untouched)

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: nginx
spec:
  selector:
    app: nginx
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80


4. /home/user/manifests/ns2/dep-api.yaml  (image is myorg/api:latest – must stay untouched)

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: api
spec:
  replicas: 1
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: api
        image: myorg/api:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080


5. /home/user/manifests/ns2/dep-nginx-2.yml  (11 lines total)

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-alt
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80


6. /home/user/manifests/ns3/job.yaml  (busybox job – untouched)

apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: demo-job
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: demo
        image: busybox
        command: ["echo", "Hello"]
      restartPolicy: Never


EXPECTED STATE AFTER SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION

Files changed:
 /home/user/manifests/dep_proxy.yaml
 /home/user/manifests/ns1/dep-nginx.yaml
 /home/user/manifests/ns2/dep-nginx-2.yml

In EACH of those three files the one original line
  image: nginx
must now read
  image: nginx:1.21

No other lines or files should differ.

New directory: /home/user/manifest_update/

New file: /home/user/manifest_update/update.log  
Exact content (order of the three lines may vary):

ns1/dep-nginx.yaml UPDATED
ns2/dep-nginx-2.yml UPDATED
dep_proxy.yaml UPDATED


New file: /home/user/manifest_update/summary.txt  
Exact content:
3
<LF>

Permissions: both new files should inherit umask-controlled defaults; any mode between 0644 and 0666 is acceptable.

The grading script will:

1. diff the three edited files against reference versions where only that line is changed.  
2. diff every untouched manifest against its original copy.  
3. verify update.log contains exactly three correctly formatted lines, each ending with newline, each path relative to /home/user/manifests.  
4. verify summary.txt contains precisely “3\n”.  
5. parse the user’s bash history to confirm at least one command used the binary names “find” and “xargs”.
